# Cover Image: Foundation Model Notes

**Generated files**:
- `figures/cover/cover-portrait.png` — PDF cover page
- `figures/cover/cover-landscape.png` — website preview / social sharing thumbnail

The PDF uses the portrait image. The web preview exports the landscape image as
`dist/cover.png`.

## Prompt

```text
Design a beautiful, modern book cover image for a technical study notes
collection titled "Foundation Model Notes" with the subtitle
"Architecture, Training, Alignment, and Evaluation."

This is a personal but rigorous set of notes covering the full stack of
modern AI: Transformer architecture, large-scale pretraining, distributed
training, RLHF, reinforcement learning, world models, and evaluation.
The visual should convey depth and craft — serious but approachable,
like well-organized research notes rather than a mass-market textbook.

STYLE:
- Clean, minimal, editorial — think O'Reilly meets MIT Press meets Bloomberg
- Color palette: deep navy (#0A1628) background fading to the book's
  signature blue (#2D8CFF) as an accent. White and light blue (#EBF5FF)
  for contrast elements.
- NO cartoon characters, NO clip art, NO stock photo vibes
- Portrait orientation (3:4 aspect ratio), suitable for a book cover

VISUAL CONCEPT — "The Architecture of Intelligence":

The central image should be an abstract, elegant visualization that
suggests the structure of a neural network / Transformer without being
a literal diagram. Think of it as:

A luminous, geometric structure rising from the bottom of the frame —
like a cathedral made of light. It is composed of:

1. ATTENTION PATTERNS — at the base, thin parallel lines converge and
   cross, forming an abstract attention matrix pattern. These lines are
   in light blue (#2D8CFF at 40% opacity), suggesting the foundational
   mechanism.

2. TRANSFORMER BLOCKS — in the middle section, the lines organize into
   repeating modular blocks stacked vertically, each block a subtle
   rounded rectangle outline. This suggests the layered architecture.
   The blocks glow softly, each slightly brighter than the one below,
   creating a sense of depth and scale.

3. EMERGENT CAPABILITY — at the top, the geometric structure dissolves
   into a soft, radiant glow — like the blocks are generating light
   that disperses outward. This represents emergent intelligence:
   the whole becomes more than the sum of its parts.

The overall shape should feel like looking up at a tower of light from
below — perspective lines converging upward, creating a sense of height
and aspiration.

TEXT PLACEMENT:

TOP AREA (above the geometric structure):
- Main title in large, bold white sans-serif:
  "Foundation Model Notes"
- The title should be the most prominent text element

MIDDLE (between title and structure, or overlaid subtly):
- Subtitle in smaller, elegant sans-serif, light blue (#2D8CFF):
  "Architecture, Training, Alignment, and Evaluation"

BOTTOM AREA (below the geometric structure):
- A thin horizontal line in light blue
- Below the line, in small muted text (#8BA4C7):
  "Transformers · Pretraining · RLHF · Reinforcement Learning · World Models"
- This tagline shows the breadth of coverage

NO author name on the cover.

VISUAL DETAILS:
- The background should have a very subtle grid or dot pattern at low
  opacity, suggesting precision and engineering rigor
- A few tiny bright nodes scattered in the geometric structure, like
  stars — suggesting key insights or breakthrough moments
- The overall mood should be: rigorous, beautiful, calm, personal
- Think of the feeling when you look up at a clear night sky and see
  the Milky Way — awe at structured complexity

COLOR CONSTRAINTS:
- Background: deep navy gradient (#0A1628 at bottom → #0F2847 at top)
- Primary accent: #2D8CFF (the book's signature blue)
- Secondary: #EBF5FF (pale blue for subtle elements)
- Text: white (#FFFFFF) for title, #8BA4C7 for secondary text
- Tiny accent: #D35400 (warm orange) for 2-3 small bright nodes only
  — these are the "moments of insight" in the structure
- NO red, NO green, NO yellow, NO purple as major elements

DO NOT:
- Literal brain illustrations
- Robot faces or humanoid figures
- Matrix/code rain (too cliché)
- Photorealistic hardware (GPUs, servers)
- Busy or cluttered composition
- Gradients that look like PowerPoint transitions
- Comic or playful style — this should feel serious and refined

QUALITY BENCHMARK:
The cover should look like it belongs on the shelf next to:
- "Deep Learning" (Goodfellow et al.) — the dark, elegant cover
- "Designing Data-Intensive Applications" (Kleppmann) — the clean
  O'Reilly aesthetic
- Nature/Science journal cover art — abstract, beautiful, scientific

The viewer's first reaction should be: "This looks like a serious,
well-crafted set of notes that I want to read."
```

## Review checklist
- [ ] Portrait orientation (3:4)
- [ ] Deep navy background, not black
- [ ] #2D8CFF blue as primary accent
- [ ] Abstract geometric structure (not a literal neural network diagram)
- [ ] Title prominently readable: "Foundation Model Notes"
- [ ] Subtitle readable: "Architecture, Training, Alignment, and Evaluation"
- [ ] Tagline at bottom showing topic breadth
- [ ] Only 2-3 tiny orange (#D35400) accent nodes
- [ ] No author name
- [ ] Serious, refined mood — not playful or introductory
- [ ] Would look credible next to Goodfellow's "Deep Learning" on a shelf
